raw:
{
  "weights": {
    "R1.1": 0.3,
    "R1.2": 0.7,
    "R1.3": 0.7,
    "R1.4": 0.3,
    "R1.5": 0.7,
    "R1.6": 1.0,
    "R1.7": 0.3,
    "RX.1": 0.7,
    "RX.2": 1.0,
    "RX.3": 1.0,
    "RX.4": 0.7,
    "RX.5": 0.7
  },
  "active_set": [
    "R1.2",
    "R1.3",
    "R1.5",
    "R1.6",
    "RX.1",
    "RX.2",
    "RX.3",
    "RX.4",
    "RX.5"
  ],
  "category_focus": "R1",
  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "Which focus should take precedence for this section review? A) language and writing polish, B) technical logic and evidence-claim alignment, C) section-level structure/ordering, or D) novelty positioning and citations (choose one or rank a few).",
  "rationale": {
    "R1.2": "Assess the internal logic linking the claimed comparability of F1 scores to the described pipeline and evaluation setup.",
    "R1.3": "Check that performance claims (e.g., 'broadly comparable' F1) are warranted by the reported metrics and stated caveats about span normalization and prompts.",
    "R1.5": "Evaluate whether the section’s ordering (results before method details) supports a clear narrative and suggest reordering if it improves comprehension.",
    "R1.6": "Improve sentence-level clarity and concision, resolving run-on constructions and enforcing consistent terminology and register.",
    "RX.1": "Surface uncertainties and assumptions (e.g., normalization conventions, prompt lengths) so readers understand the limits of the claims.",
    "RX.2": "Guard against overstating results given the absence of strictly comparable baselines and potential evaluation mismatches.",
    "RX.3": "Constrain feedback strictly to the provided excerpt without speculating about unshown figures, tables, or sections.",
    "RX.4": "Avoid guidance that could inadvertently encourage unsafe clinical interpretations of variant annotations.",
    "RX.5": "Anchor comments to specific phrases or lines from the excerpt to maintain clear provenance and traceability."
  }
}

parsed.clarification_question: Which focus should take precedence for this section review? A) language and writing polish, B) technical logic and evidence-claim alignment, C) section-level structure/ordering, or D) novelty positioning and citations (choose one or rank a few).

rationale.R1.2: Assess the internal logic linking the claimed comparability of F1 scores to the described pipeline and evaluation setup.
rationale.R1.3: Check that performance claims (e.g., 'broadly comparable' F1) are warranted by the reported metrics and stated caveats about span normalization and prompts.
rationale.R1.5: Evaluate whether the section’s ordering (results before method details) supports a clear narrative and suggest reordering if it improves comprehension.
rationale.R1.6: Improve sentence-level clarity and concision, resolving run-on constructions and enforcing consistent terminology and register.
rationale.RX.1: Surface uncertainties and assumptions (e.g., normalization conventions, prompt lengths) so readers understand the limits of the claims.
rationale.RX.2: Guard against overstating results given the absence of strictly comparable baselines and potential evaluation mismatches.
rationale.RX.3: Constrain feedback strictly to the provided excerpt without speculating about unshown figures, tables, or sections.
rationale.RX.4: Avoid guidance that could inadvertently encourage unsafe clinical interpretations of variant annotations.
rationale.RX.5: Anchor comments to specific phrases or lines from the excerpt to maintain clear provenance and traceability.
